Package cc.quaternion.potshaper

Class PotFont

java.lang.Object
cc.quaternion.potshaper.PotFont
public class PotFont extends Object
Represents an immutable font configuration for text styling within the PotShaper library.

This class encapsulates font properties such as family, size, weight, style, and color. It provides a fluent API for creating modified instances, following an immutable builder pattern. Predefined constants are available for common use cases like titles, body text, and code.

Since:
1.0
Author:
Quaternion8192

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT

      public static final PotFont DEFAULT
      The default font configuration.

      This constant uses the "Calibri" family with a size of 18 points.

    • TITLE

      public static final PotFont TITLE
      A font configuration suitable for main titles.

      This constant uses the "Calibri Light" family with a size of 44 points.

    • SUBTITLE

      public static final PotFont SUBTITLE
      A font configuration suitable for subtitles.

      This constant uses the "Calibri" family with a size of 32 points.

    • BODY

      public static final PotFont BODY
      A font configuration suitable for standard body text.

      This constant uses the "Calibri" family with a size of 18 points.

    • CODE

      public static final PotFont CODE
      A font configuration suitable for displaying source code.

      This constant uses the monospaced "Consolas" family with a size of 14 points.

  • Constructor Details

    • PotFont

      public PotFont(String family, double size)
      Constructs a new font configuration with the specified family and size.

      All other style properties (bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, color, spacing, language) are set to their default values (false or null). If the provided family is null, the family defaults to "Calibri". If the provided size is not positive, the size defaults to 18.

      Parameters:
      family - the name of the font family, or null to use the default "Calibri"
      size - the font size in points; must be positive or will default to 18

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static PotFont of(String family, double size)
      Creates a new font configuration with the specified family and size.

      This is a static convenience method equivalent to the public constructor PotFont(String, double).

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont customFont = PotFont.of("Arial", 24.0);
      Parameters:
      family - the name of the font family, or null to use the default "Calibri"
      size - the font size in points; must be positive or will default to 18
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the given family and size
      See Also:
      • PotFont(String, double)

    • size

      public static PotFont size(double size)
      Creates a new font configuration with the specified size, using the default family.

      The font family is set to the default "Calibri".

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont largeDefault = PotFont.size(36.0);
      Parameters:
      size - the font size in points; must be positive or will default to 18
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the default family and the given size

    • family

      public static PotFont family(String family)
      Creates a new font configuration with the specified family, using the default size.

      The font size is set to the default 18 points.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont timesFont = PotFont.family("Times New Roman");
      Parameters:
      family - the name of the font family, or null to use the default "Calibri"
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the given family and default size

    • create

      public static PotFont create()
      Creates a new font configuration with all default properties.

      This method returns a font equivalent to the DEFAULT constant, using the "Calibri" family and a size of 18 points.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont defaultFont = PotFont.create();
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with default family and size
      See Also:
      • DEFAULT

    • withFamily

      public PotFont withFamily(String family)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified family, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont original = PotFont.DEFAULT;
 PotFont modified = original.withFamily("Verdana");
      Parameters:
      family - the new font family name, or null to use the default "Calibri"
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated family

    • withSize

      public PotFont withSize(double size)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified size, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont original = PotFont.TITLE;
 PotFont smallerTitle = original.withSize(32.0);
      Parameters:
      size - the new font size in points; must be positive or will default to 18
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated size

    • bold

      public PotFont bold()
      Returns a new font configuration with the bold property set to true.

      This is a convenience method equivalent to bold(true).

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont boldBody = PotFont.BODY.bold();
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with bold enabled
      See Also:
      • bold(boolean)

    • bold

      public PotFont bold(boolean bold)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified bold property, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont original = PotFont.CODE;
 PotFont nonBoldCode = original.bold(false);
      Parameters:
      bold - true to enable bold styling, false to disable it
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated bold property

    • italic

      public PotFont italic()
      Returns a new font configuration with the italic property set to true.

      This is a convenience method equivalent to italic(true).

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont italicTitle = PotFont.TITLE.italic();
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with italic enabled
      See Also:
      • italic(boolean)

    • italic

      public PotFont italic(boolean italic)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified italic property, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont original = PotFont.SUBTITLE;
 PotFont nonItalicSubtitle = original.italic(false);
      Parameters:
      italic - true to enable italic styling, false to disable it
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated italic property

    • underline

      public PotFont underline()
      Returns a new font configuration with the underline property set to true.

      This is a convenience method equivalent to underline(true).

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont underlinedBody = PotFont.BODY.underline();
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with underline enabled
      See Also:
      • underline(boolean)

    • underline

      public PotFont underline(boolean underline)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified underline property, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont original = PotFont.DEFAULT;
 PotFont notUnderlined = original.underline(false);
      Parameters:
      underline - true to enable underline styling, false to disable it
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated underline property

    • strikethrough

      public PotFont strikethrough()
      Returns a new font configuration with the strikethrough property set to true.

      This is a convenience method equivalent to strikethrough(true).

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont struckThrough = PotFont.CODE.strikethrough();
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with strikethrough enabled
      See Also:
      • strikethrough(boolean)

    • strikethrough

      public PotFont strikethrough(boolean strikethrough)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified strikethrough property, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont original = PotFont.BODY;
 PotFont notStruckThrough = original.strikethrough(false);
      Parameters:
      strikethrough - true to enable strikethrough styling, false to disable it
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated strikethrough property

    • withColor

      public PotFont withColor(PotColor color)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified text color, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont redTitle = PotFont.TITLE.withColor(PotColor.RED);
      Parameters:
      color - the new text color, or null to remove any specific color
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated color

    • color

      public PotFont color(PotColor color)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified text color, preserving all other properties.

      This method is an alias for withColor(PotColor) and is provided for API fluency.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont blueCode = PotFont.CODE.color(PotColor.BLUE);
      Parameters:
      color - the new text color, or null to remove any specific color
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated color
      See Also:
      • withColor(PotColor)

    • withCharacterSpacing

      public PotFont withCharacterSpacing(double spacing)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified character spacing, preserving all other properties.

      Character spacing adjusts the horizontal space between characters in points. This method does not modify the current instance.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont spacedFont = PotFont.DEFAULT.withCharacterSpacing(1.5);
      Parameters:
      spacing - the character spacing in points
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated character spacing

    • withLanguage

      public PotFont withLanguage(String language)
      Returns a new font configuration with the specified language tag, preserving all other properties.

      This method does not modify the current instance. The language tag can be used by text layout engines for locale-specific typographic features, such as hyphenation or glyph selection. If the provided language is null, the resulting font will have no explicit language setting.

      Usage Example

      
 PotFont japaneseFont = PotFont.BODY.withLanguage("ja-JP");
      Parameters:
      language - the language tag (e.g., "zh-CN", "en-US"), or null to clear the setting
      Returns:
      a new PotFont instance with the updated language

    • getFamily

      public String getFamily()
      Returns the font family name.
      Returns:
      the font family name, never null

    • getSize

      public double getSize()
      Returns the font size in points.
      Returns:
      the font size in points

    • isBold

      public boolean isBold()
      Returns whether the font is bold.
      Returns:
      true if the font is bold, false otherwise

    • isItalic

      public boolean isItalic()
      Returns whether the font is italic.
      Returns:
      true if the font is italic, false otherwise

    • isUnderline

      public boolean isUnderline()
      Returns whether the font is underlined.
      Returns:
      true if the font is underlined, false otherwise

    • isStrikethrough

      public boolean isStrikethrough()
      Returns whether the font has strikethrough.
      Returns:
      true if the font has strikethrough, false otherwise

    • getColor

      public PotColor getColor()
      Returns the text color.
      Returns:
      the text color, or null if no explicit color is set

    • getCharacterSpacing

      public Double getCharacterSpacing()
      Returns the character spacing.
      Returns:
      the character spacing in points, or null if no explicit spacing is set

    • getLanguage

      public String getLanguage()
      Returns the language tag.
      Returns:
      the language tag (e.g., "en-US"), or null if no explicit language is set

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object o)
      Compares this font configuration to the specified object for equality.

      Two PotFont instances are considered equal if all their property values are equal. This includes family, size, bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, color, character spacing, and language. Floating-point comparison for size uses exact comparison via Double.compare(double, double).

      Overrides:
      equals in class Object
      Parameters:
      o - the object to compare with
      Returns:
      true if the objects are equal, false otherwise

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this font configuration.

      The hash code is computed from all property values.

      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      a hash code value for this object

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a string representation of this font configuration.

      The string includes all non-default property values in a human-readable format. Properties with default values (e.g., false for bold) are omitted for brevity.

      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      a string representation of this object
